nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Waiting for someone to change before you let yourself heal is its own kind of trap. The resentmentВ lives in every moment since where you stayed silent, managed their reaction, and left your own needs sitting on the floor.

Your voice might shake and it might be really hard, but the alternative is hard too, and we can do hard things (thanks for that one, @glennondoyle).

#boundaries #selfawareness #relationships

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
The moment you stop trying to fix your partner is the moment your relationship actually starts working.

HereвҖҷs what actually shifts everything: learning to see your partnerвҖҷs resistance as information, not defiance.

The couples who last arenвҖҷt the ones who never trigger each other. TheyвҖҷre the ones who got curious about what lives underneath the trigger instead of trying to eliminate it. They learned that your partnerвҖҷs вҖңвҖқflawsвҖқвҖқ are usually their survival strategies.

Your nervous system wants safety, but your soul wants growth. The magic happens when you can hold both without making your partner responsible for either.

#relationships #love #growth

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
HereвҖҷs what nobody tells you about вҖңrelationship goalsвҖқ: the magic isnвҖҷt in never breaking. ItвҖҷs in how beautifully you rebuild. вңЁ

You can tell everything about a couple not by watching them at brunch, but by watching what happens when someone messes up. Do they retreat into separate corners or find their way back to each otherвҖҷs humanity? вқӨпёҸ

The couples who make it arenвҖҷt the ones who never disconnect. TheyвҖҷre the ones whoвҖҷve learned that disconnection is just the comma, not the period. Real intimacy lives in the aftermath when someone says вҖңI was wrongвҖқ and actually means it. When you can be pissed off without making it dangerous. 

ItвҖҷs a fools game to romanticize the highlight reel. The measure of love isnвҖҷt in the absence of mess, itвҖҷs in your willingness to clean it up together. Again and again. Without keeping score. 

#relationships #love #connection

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
You can have all the вҖңrightвҖқ tools and still feel like youвҖҷre barely holding it together because youвҖҷre trying to show up for hard conversations on an empty tank. We talk a lot about emotional skills, but not enough about the basics that actually support them: sleep, energy, hormones, nourishment. 

ItвҖҷs a lot easier to stay grounded when your body isnвҖҷt already struggling behind the scenes.

Use code BAYA25 for a discount off your membership: https://functionhealth.com/bayavoce

In collaboration with Function Health.

#mentalhealth #selfregulation #wellness

#breakup #healing #growth

nombre: Baya Voce | Relationship Repair Expert | Columbia University
bio:
If you want a healthy relationship, repair is non-negotiable. I help you learn how.
Supervision w/ Esther Perel
Couples Research @columbia @maps__org
descripcion:
Comment EFFORT for a free pdf teaching you what to do in this scenario. 
.
One of you is pushing for change, the other is holding space for what already feels okay. This isnвҖҷt incompatibility, itвҖҷs a common rhythm many couples get stuck in.

One partner feels alone in the effort to grow, the other feels pressured just for being themselves. ItвҖҷs not about whoвҖҷs right or wrong, but about seeing how different ways of loving show up.

If youвҖҷre the one chasing progress, try noticing the value in whatвҖҷs already here without mistaking it for being stuck.

If youвҖҷre the one content with the now, take a small step toward curiosity and ask questions before the tension builds.

This dynamic isnвҖҷt a problem, itвҖҷs a chance to learn how to love across difference instead of push against it.

#relationships #growth #connection

bio:
Helping you unlock your potential through masterful communication рҹҡҖ
Free 2 Hour Training рҹ‘Ү
descripcion:
Avoid using this hand gesture!

Comment 'youtube' for the full video breakdown.

This is called the Steeple (or I call it the Mr Burns) and it makes you come across a bit "schemey" and dominant.

I see many speakers default to this hand gesture when speaking to an audience, and when something becomes a default, it becomes non-functional. 

Use it occasionally for effect but don't get stuck in it!

#bodylanguage #communicationskills

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion: Confident People Avoid Eye Contact! | @thediaryofaceopodcast

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
The fastest way to kill momentum is to keep measuring your life against someone elseвҖҷs highlight reel.

You canвҖҷt build confidence while constantly checking whoвҖҷs ahead of you. You canвҖҷt hear your own ideas when your brain is full of everyone elseвҖҷs timeline, numbers, relationships, career moves, body, house, success. 

Comparison doesnвҖҷt make you better. It makes you distracted.

Most people you envy are just further along in a process you canвҖҷt see. TheyвҖҷve failed privately more times than youвҖҷve probably watched publicly. And meanwhile, while youвҖҷre busy wondering why youвҖҷre not there yet, youвҖҷre missing the fact that youвҖҷre already becoming someone you once hoped to be.

The people who actually succeed long term usually arenвҖҷt obsessed with competing. TheyвҖҷre obsessed with improving. 

Quietly. Repeatedly. Consistently.

Your path was never supposed to look identical to someone elseвҖҷs. Different timing does not mean failure. Different strengths do not mean less value.

Comparison convinces you to stop before your life has even had the chance to compound.
WhatвҖҷs something youвҖҷve achieved lately that a past version of you would be proud of?

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
Your teen says, вҖңNothing happened.вҖқ

Their face says otherwise. рҹҳ…

On @jenniferhudsonshow I broke down the subtle body language and behavioral cues that can reveal when a teen isnвҖҷt telling the whole truth...and why catching the lie isnвҖҷt always the goal.

The real skill? Creating enough trust that they tell you the truth in the first place.

Have you ever instantly known your teen was hiding something? рҹ‘Җ

#ParentingTips #BodyLanguage #CommunicationSkills #TeenParenting #JenniferHudsonShow

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
For years, I got lost in metrics.

Views. Followers. Revenue. Opportunities. Growth. Achievement. I kept chasing the next number, convinced that once I hit a certain milestone, I'd finally feel successful.

The problem was that every time I reached a goal, the target moved. There was always another benchmark, another comparison, another reason to feel like I wasn't quite there yet.

It was exhausting.

The shift that changed everything was surprisingly simple: I stopped asking, "How am I doing?" and started asking, "How can I help?"

Now, my favorite metric is whether I was useful.

Did I share something that made someone's life easier? Did I help someone navigate a challenge? Did I give a person the confidence, information, or encouragement they needed to take the next step?

Ironically, focusing less on myself and more on being helpful brought me more fulfillment than any metric ever did.

Numbers can tell you how much attention you're getting. They can't tell you whether you're making a difference.

These days, my definition of success is simple: leave people better than you found them.

What's a metric you've stopped caring about as you've gotten older?

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
Being an introvert is not a bad thing.

Somewhere along the way, a lot of introverts got the message that they should be more outgoing, more social, more talkative, more extroverted.
But youвҖҷre not a broken extrovert.

YouвҖҷre not an extrovert who needs more confidence.

YouвҖҷre not an extrovert who hasnвҖҷt come out of their shell yet.

YouвҖҷre an introvert.

And when you stop trying to be someone else, you can start building confidence, connection, and success in a way that actually works for you.

Comment INTROVERT and IвҖҷll send you my training on how to harness your personality as a strength.

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion: Never Ever Say вҖңNice To Meet YouвҖқ | @jenniferhudsonshow

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
The secret formula to a great presentation is finding the balance between inspiration and information, because no matter how smart, accomplished, or knowledgeable you are, people will stop listening the second they feel like theyвҖҷre being talked at instead of spoken to. 

PS - if you like tips like this this is just a SAMPLE of what you can expect from my coaching cohort. Comment COACH if you'd like access to our exclusive coaching program, now! 

The presentations that actually leave an impact are the ones that teach people something valuable while also making them feel curious, inspired, challenged, excited, or understood in the process. Facts and data might earn attention for a moment, but emotion is what makes people remember you afterward. 

The best presenters understand that their job isnвҖҷt just to transfer information onto a slide deck вҖ” itвҖҷs to create connection, shift perspective, and make an audience genuinely care about the message theyвҖҷre hearing.

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
One hand gesture can instantly make you look more confident.

ItвҖҷs called the steeple. рҹ‘Ҷ

When someone lightly touches their fingertips together, creating a вҖңchurch steepleвҖқ shape, it often signals confidence, certainty, and authority.

On @jenniferhudsonshow I shared why this is one of the most powerful nonverbal cues in body language. 

The key? Use it naturally. Forced confidence looks forced.

Your body speaks before you do.

Have you ever noticed someone using the steeple in a meeting, interview, or negotiation? рҹ‘Җ

#BodyLanguage #ConfidenceTips #CommunicationSkills #JenniferHudsonShow #Leadership

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
For years, I thought being вҖңbad at networkingвҖқ was going to ruin my career.

I hated small talk. Hated walking into rooms full of strangers. Hated trying to sound impressive. I thought success belonged to the loudest person in the room, the smoothest talker, the person who could magically turn every conversation into an opportunity.
Meanwhile, I was overthinking every interaction and replaying conversations in my head for hours afterward.

What changed everything was realizing people donвҖҷt actually connect through perfect small talk. They connect through feeling seen. Through curiosity.

Through real conversations. Through remembering details. Through making someone feel comfortable instead of trying to perform.

The second I stopped trying to вҖңnetworkвҖқ and started trying to genuinely understand people, everything changed. My career grew faster.

My relationships became deeper. Opportunities came from authenticity instead of pretending to be someone I wasnвҖҷt.

Turns out, being bad at surface-level conversation wasnвҖҷt the thing holding me back. Believing I had to become someone else to succeed was.

Comment CONVERSATION and IвҖҷll send you the pre-order link to my book on creating deeper connections in every area of your life.

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
THESE Are Biggest Interview Mistakes YOUвҖҷRE MAKING!

вҖңI donвҖҷt understand. I thought the interview went really well.вҖқ

Then I ask a few questions.

Did you actually answer the question they asked?

Did you give specific examples?
Did you explain the impact of your work?
Did you ask thoughtful questions back?

Usually the answer is... not really.
Most people think interviews are about proving theyвҖҷre qualified.

TheyвҖҷre about helping someone picture what itвҖҷs like to work with you.
If your answers are too long, too vague, overly rehearsed, or sound like a rГ©sumГ© being read out loud, youвҖҷre making it harder for the interviewer to see your value.

The frustrating part is that these mistakes are often invisible to the person making them.
ThatвҖҷs why so many talented people leave interviews feeling confident... and never get the call.

The good news? Interviewing is a skill. And skills can be learned.

Comment INTERVIEW and IвҖҷll send you training on how to answer questions confidently, communicate your value, and stand out from the competition.

descripcion:
"I was raised with the Golden Rule. And it got me in trouble." @vvanedwards on why "treat others as you'd want to be treated" can quietly keep you self-focused, especially at work, especially under pressure.

Her fix: the Platinum Rule. Treat people as THEY want to be treated. Ask yourself, "If I were them, what would be worrying me right now?" That tiny mental shift is the real unlock for empathy.

Which rule have you been running on? 

New episode link in bio.

#ThinkFastTalkSmart #PlatinumRule #CommunicationTips #PodcastClips

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
People see the career now! They donвҖҷt see the experiments...

The late nights testing hooks that flopped. The videos no one watched. The moments I thought maybe I just wasnвҖҷt вҖңnaturally talentedвҖқ enough to make this work.

At one point I literally rubbed popcorn on my arms for a video idea. No strategy. No dignity. Just commitment to seeing what worked. рҹҳӮ
ThatвҖҷs the part nobody talks about. Building a career online isnвҖҷt one big breakthrough moment. 

ItвҖҷs hundreds of weird little experiments stacked on top of each other until eventually something clicks.

Every opportunity I have now came from being willing to look ridiculous before I looked successful.

And honestly? I think thatвҖҷs true for almost every dream career. The people who make it are usually the ones willing to keep experimenting long after everyone else gets embarrassed and quits.

WhatвҖҷs the weirdest thing youвҖҷve ever done trying to chase a goal?

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
One of the reasons social skills feel so overwhelming is that people try to learn all of them at once.

They think they need to become more confident, more charismatic, better at small talk, better at networking, better at public speaking, and better at reading body language... all at the same time.

No wonder it feels impossible.

If you want someone to show you exactly what to work on first, comment **COACH** and we'll send you the details about our coaching program.

The people who become socially skilled rarely transform overnight. They focus on micro social skills.

They practice making eye contact for one extra second. They learn how to ask a better follow-up question.

They work on remembering names. They get comfortable introducing themselves to one new person. They learn how to enter a group conversation without feeling awkward.

Each skill seems small on its own, but together they create massive change.

The goal isn't to become a completely different person. The goal is to get comfortable with one new social behavior at a time until it becomes part of who you are.

Confidence is usually the result of competence.

And competence is built through small, repeatable wins.

descripcion:
I always knew I wanted to help people. I just didn't always know how.
For years I felt like everyone else got a manual for human connection that I somehow missed. Conversations felt awkward and I constantly felt like people were mad at me.
Then I started learning the actual science of people skills вҖ” and it changed my life. Not slowly. Not vaguely. It rewired how I walked into a room, how I connected, how I was seen. And the wildest part? How fast it happened. I watched my whole life shift once I had the right tools, and I've spent 18 years since proving these frameworks work вҖ” with Fortune 500 leaders, educators, therapists, and entrepreneurs. In the last two years the demand for soft skills has increased exponentially. Not only for individuals who want to learn how to communicate with confidence but also inside companies. Trainings, workshops and lunch and learns on communication are in demand in the Age of AI as it is the best form of career insurance.
That's why I built the People Coach Certification. So I could hand these tools to the people who are already out there helping others вҖ” coaches, managers, teachers, therapists, facilitators. I'm looking for 25 people for Cohort 2. We now have 30 certified coaches from Cohort 1 and I am ready for our next group! I'm looking for 2 types of people: 1. Intrapreneurs - people in corporate looking to do communication coaching in their company or in their role. 2. Entrepreneurs - life coaches, therapists, social workers, counselors, and executive coaches who want to bring this curriculum into their current practice.
This is a rigorous, science-backed certification: our PEOPLE framework, the D3 Lesson Loopв„ў, proprietary assessments, done-for-you workshop kits, live sessions with me, and a private community of serious professionals.
For this reason this is application only. I'm looking for folks with the motivation to use communication as a force for good, alignment with our mission, and the drive to actually do the work. When you're accepted, you'll know you're standing among a high-caliber group who take this as seriously as you do.
Our first cohort just produced 30 certified People Coaches вҖ” and watching them go out

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
Like behavior tips like this? Comment COACH for access to my signature coaching program with much more on how to put people at ease! 

One of the easiest ways to make someone instantly light up in conversation: pay attention to their phone screen.

People put what they love most right in front of them вҖ” their dog, their kids, a favorite place, a hobby, a memory they never want to forget. If you notice it and bring it up naturally, you skip surface-level small talk and go straight into something they actually care about.

вҖңWait, is that your golden retriever?вҖқ
вҖңWhere was that photo taken?вҖқ
вҖңYour kid is adorable вҖ” how old are they?вҖқ

The fastest way to be interesting is to make people feel seen.

bio:
Harvard Instructor & Behavioral Researcher
рҹ—ЈпёҸ Helping you master communication
Author, Captivate & Cues | DM вҖңQUIZвҖқ for Your Blind Spot
descripcion:
What is a contrarianвҖҰ and could you actually be one?

Contrarians tend to challenge social norms, think independently, and question things most people automatically accept. But socially, this can either make you incredibly interestingвҖҰ or accidentally difficult to connect with.

A lot of highly intelligent people donвҖҷt realize the way they communicate can come across as combative instead of curious.

The difference usually comes down to delivery.

This was a question from one of our incredible students at People School, our signature program! Comment SCHOOL for access to our live sessions and more tips - like this one!

bio:
Lawyer вҶ’ VP вҶ’ Founder
Your team isn't the problem. Your leadership system is.
I help $1M+ founders/CEOs lead without being the bottleneck.
Start Hereрҹ‘Ү
descripcion:
Your team isnвҖҷt slow. TheyвҖҷre not disengaged. TheyвҖҷre not the wrong hires.

They just donвҖҷt feel safe making the call without you.

HereвҖҷs what that looks like in practice:

1. They ask you questions they already know the answer to.

2. They send messages that start with вҖңjust wanted to check with you before I...вҖқ

3. A decision that should take 10 minutes waits until your next 1:1.

4. They present options instead of a recommendation.

5. They cc you on emails that donвҖҷt require your input.

6. They redo work after your feedback even when the first version was fine.

7. They go quiet in meetings when the topic is outside their explicit lane.

8. They tell you what happened after itвҖҷs already done, never before.

9. When something goes sideways, the first call is to you.

10. They say вҖңI wasnвҖҷt sure if I shouldвҖқ more than вҖңI handled it.вҖқ

11. Your calendar is full of conversations that exist because your team doesnвҖҷt trust their own judgment yet.

This isnвҖҷt a people problem. ItвҖҷs a missing layer in how your business is built.

I wrote the full breakdown on Substack, whatвҖҷs creating this dynamic and the 4 things that change it.

Comment SUBSTACK and IвҖҷll DM you the link.

#leadershipcommunication

descripcion:
Resourcefulness is one of 3 values we have at my company.
Which means when something goes sideways вҖ” and it always does вҖ” my team doesnвҖҷt stop and wait for me.

They find a way.

Because we defined what resourcefulness actually looks like in my business. 

We made it a standard, not just a word on a slide. And I built safety so they could act on it without my sign-off.

By constantly reinforcing the value, recognizing the people who practice it, and giving them the safety to execute, I can walk away knowing the work will get done the way I need it to be done.

ThatвҖҷs not hoping they get it. ThatвҖҷs infrastructure.

#leadershipcommunication
